jobs.statement: When I am running multiple active projects and agents, I want a single project-scoped workspace with adaptable canvases and tabs, so I can stay oriented and reduce context-switch overhead.
When I talk to Lev in voice or text, I want the system to respond with structured interactive UI instead of raw logs, so I can review and act faster.
When a privileged action is requested, I want clear policy-gated checkpoints and approvals, so I can trust execution without accidental destructive behavior.
When renderer technology changes (Thesys to AgentPing renderer), I want UI behavior to remain stable through a shared DSL contract, so we can evolve implementation without UX regression.
When daemon, index, and transport health drift, I want a live control-plane status view with diagnostics and repair actions, so I can recover quickly without terminal spelunking.
When config behavior is unexpected, I want scope-by-scope provenance and safe edit controls, so I can fix settings without creating hidden side effects.
When I evaluate system trustworthiness, I want explicit validation gate outcomes and architecture pass status, so I can decide if execution should proceed.
When coordinating remote workers, I want wave-level visibility with approvals and recovery controls, so I can manage throughput and failures safely.
When I feel overloaded by complexity, I want layered views and mode-specific summaries, so I can stay in flow without losing critical context.
